title: archette
text: GARCHETTE is a minimal implementation of the GARCH(1,1) model with zero mean and Gaussian noise.

title: archette
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/xiaochuany/archette.git


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages.

title: archette
text:
How to use

import numpy as np
from archette import GARCHETTE


y = np.random.randn(20)
mod = GARCHETTE()
mod.fit(y)

<archette.core.GARCHETTE>


One can get inspect the fit params: a triplet of omega, alpha and beta as in
\[
\begin{align*}
Y_t &= \sigma_t W_t \\
\sigma_t^2 &=  \omega + \alpha Y_{t-1}^2 + \beta \sigma^2_{t-1}
\end{align*}
\]

mod.params

array([1.46162489e+00, 0.00000000e+00, 4.34165554e-16])


The conditional variance of the fit model is computed by the recursion
\[
\hat{\sigma}_t^2 = \hat{\omega} + \hat{\alpha} + \hat{\beta} \hat{\sigma}_{t-1}^2
\]
which depends entirely on the unobserved \(\sigma_0^2\). The model sets a sensible default value for it.

mod.vs

array([1.6637509 , 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489,
       1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489,
       1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489,
       1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489])


The standardised residual is deduced from the conditional variance
\[
r_t = \frac{y_t}{\hat{\sigma}_t}
\]

mod.std_resids

array([ 1.83606806,  0.40202783, -0.05913285, -0.47174874, -0.59056739,
       -2.20575562,  0.71528908,  0.26541164, -0.20129438,  1.33214741,
       -0.50849529,  0.2951227 , -0.84969512,  1.53542218,  0.44267321,
       -1.95023795, -1.68947028, -0.23377285, -0.18693467,  0.7959041 ])


Finally, one can forecast the condtional variance and simulate the process with the fit parameters with a given horizon

mod.forecast_vs(horizon=5)

array([1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489, 1.46162489])



mod.simulate(horizon=5, method="bootstrap", n_rep=2, seed=1)

array([[-2.66670736,  0.35679649, -1.02726169, -0.24336023,  1.61053531],
       [ 0.35679649, -2.66670736, -2.35779243,  2.21976368, -2.04253036]])

title: core
text:
source

GARCHETTE

 GARCHETTE ()

simple garch model

source


GARCHETTE.fit

 GARCHETTE.fit (y:numpy.ndarray)

fit y to garch

source


GARCHETTE.vs

 GARCHETTE.vs ()

property: estimated conditional variance, same shape as y

source


GARCHETTE.nll

 GARCHETTE.nll (params)

negative log likelihood of the series at the given params

source


GARCHETTE.std_resids

 GARCHETTE.std_resids ()

property: estimated standardized residual

source


GARCHETTE.forecast_vs

 GARCHETTE.forecast_vs (horizon:int)

forecast conditional variance in the horizon

source


GARCHETTE.simulate

 GARCHETTE.simulate (horizon:int,
                     method:Literal['bootstrap','simulate']='simulate',
                     n_rep=1000, seed=42)











Type
Default
Details




horizon
int

path length


method
Literal
simulate
“bootstrap” resamples from past std_resids; “simulate” simulates gaussian nosie


n_rep
int
1000
number of repetitions


seed
int
42



Returns
ndarray






y = np.random.randn(600) * np.arange(600)
mod = GARCHETTE().fit(y)


from arch import arch_model


mod_arch = arch_model(y, mean="zero", rescale=False).fit(disp="off")

The model is mis-specified here. arch and archette leads to very different parameters estimates with similar loss.

print(f'parameter fit by achette {mod.params}: loss {mod.nll(mod.params)}')
print(f'parameter fit by arch    {mod_arch.params}: loss {mod.nll(mod_arch.params)}')

parameter fit by achette [155.25608105   0.1802956    0.8197044 ]: loss 7248.148758000214
parameter fit by arch    omega       33.361983
alpha[1]     0.057017
beta[1]      0.942983
Name: params, dtype: float64: loss 7299.636859660716


Now simulate a new path with mod.params as parameter. Both arch and archette leads to similar parameter estiamtes.

yy = mod.simulate(600, n_rep=1).squeeze()
modyy = GARCHETTE().fit(yy)
modyy_arch = arch_model(yy,mean="zero",rescale=False).fit(disp='off')


modyy.params, modyy_arch.params

(array([178.92960969,   0.18508077,   0.80731124]),
 omega       167.995919
 alpha[1]      0.157700
 beta[1]       0.827654
 Name: params, dtype: float64)
